commands.author: qingminglong
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates shared command-catalog presentation helpers, the WinUI chat composer slash popup, and shared tests to render grouped Session/Model/Tools/Agents slash-command rows with styling and auto-scroll.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows bucket-ordered flattening can put an earlier display bucket ahead of the global top search result. I did not run the Windows UI, but the ranking and selection paths are clear from the diff, current main, and the inspected screenshot.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair is plausible because the blocker is a concrete selection-order bug in the slash palette plus missing shared presentation coverage.

target.title: fix(qqbot): strip echoed inbound metadata from replies
target.body:
Closes #97980

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where QQ Bot users could see OpenClaw's internal inbound metadata at the start of replies, such as `Conversation info (untrusted metadata)` and `Sender (untrusted metadata)` JSON blocks, before the actual assistant response.

## Why This Change Was Made

QQ Bot now runs outbound-visible text through a plugin-owned sanitizer that keeps the existing assistant-visible text cleanup and strips leading echoed inbound metadata blocks before delivery. The same sanitizer is used by the QQ outbound adapter surface and by the direct QQ gateway dispatch path, so normal final replies and buffered/tool progress text share the protection.

The direct QQ gateway path now sanitizes block reply text before silent-reply classification as well as before delivery. That keeps metadata-prefixed `NO_REPLY` and `[SKIP]` control replies suppressed instead of stripping the metadata after the silence check and accidentally sending the control token.

The change stays local to the QQ Bot plugin and does not alter the prompt envelope sent to the model.

## User Impact

QQ Bot replies no longer expose technical OpenClaw context blocks to end users when a model echoes the inbound prompt metadata. Users see only the intended assistant response text, and metadata-prefixed silent replies remain silent.

## Evidence

Duplicate PR gate before implementation:

```text
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt -- "97980"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt -- "内部元数据 渲染 用户聊天框"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt -- "qqbot Conversation info Sender untrusted metadata"
```

Observed result: no open PRs matched #97980 or the QQ Bot metadata leak keywords.

Focused tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts
# passed: 2 files, 32 tests
```

The focused tests cover both the QQ Bot message adapter sanitizer and the direct gateway dispatch path, including a metadata-prefixed normal reply and a metadata-prefixed `NO_REPLY` block that must remain suppressed.

Formatting, diff hygiene, and local review:

```text
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/qqbot/src/engine/utils/visible-text.ts extensions/qqbot/src/channel.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.ts extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts
# passed

git diff --check
# passed

.agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local
# passed with no reported output
```

Real sanitizer proof using the production QQ Bot visible-text helper with the issue's metadata shape:

```text
node --import tsx --input-type=module
# input: leading Conversation info + Sender JSON metadata blocks followed by "final answer"
# output: { "sanitized": "final answer" }
```

Follow-up after ClawSweeper review:

```text
# ClawSweeper reported that metadata-prefixed NO_REPLY/[SKIP] could be stripped after the silent check.
# Fixed by sanitizing block reply text before silent classification and delivery planning.
# Added regression coverage for metadata-prefixed NO_REPLY remaining suppressed.
```

Not tested: a live QQ Bot account conversation. The proof covers the plugin outbound adapter sanitizer and the direct gateway dispatch path with focused tests, but no redacted live QQ Bot output, log, screenshot, or recording was available from this environment.
target.author: qingminglong

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
